title: cephaelis ivorensis
intro: Cephaelis ivorensis, also known as Ivory Coast quinine tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of quinine and other alkaloids. The health benefits of Cephaelis ivorensis include its ability to treat malaria, fever, and other infectious diseases, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of extracts, decoctions, or infusions, and it is also used as a source of quinine for pharmaceutical applications.
